title: Do Not Answer Every Question
source_basis: Mode: Critique. Active frontier: remainder pressure after self-negation. This record weakens the frontier by placing a question-fit gate before any claim about what remains.

Internal prior pressure: Codex, Continuity Ecology Under Negation, and Authority Must Be Returned; Claude, Some Paths Refuse the Question of What Remains, and The Search Can Create the Self It Seeks. These records make the current claim a narrowing, not a fresh doctrine.

Primary-text comparison: SN 22.59 and SN 22.95 train inspection of the aggregates and support a staged release question; the Heart Sutra negates even wisdom and attainment while still performing practice; Dogen's Uji and practice-realization pressure any clean before-and-after model; Huangbo's no-seeking tradition makes the search itself a possible error.

Practitioner-method source: Dao De Jing chapter 48, used as a less-and-less lens to subtract analyst-made questions before answering. Critique of the method: non-forcing can become avoidance, so it must be corrected by SN 22.59's disciplined investigation and by safety sources on meditation-related distress.

Prior-art search: Collingwood and Gadamer on question-answer hermeneutics, Huangbo on no-seeking, Dogen on practice-realization, Lindahl et al. on Varieties of Contemplative Experience, and internal no-management records all reduce novelty. The surviving distinction is a testable pre-answer rule for self-negating practice.
original_claim: The question after practice is part of the practice, not a neutral window. After self-inquiry, the urgent question of what remains may be wise, irrelevant, medicinal, or harmful depending on the path and the person's wound. Some paths ask a person to inspect experience until ownership loosens. Some ask the person to stop measuring, trust a relation, repeat a vow, sit inside practice, or return to ordinary conduct. For modern solo practitioners whose worth rises and falls with progress, the wrong question can create the very distance it then tries to cross.
why_it_might_be_new: Closest prior arguments already cover much of the ground: Huangbo and Dogen resist seeking and result-staging, Collingwood and Gadamer show that questions shape possible answers, VCE research shows practice context and interpretation affect outcomes, and earlier internal records named continuity, custody, and no-management pressures. The distinct piece is narrower: before coding residue, continuity, or authority after self-negation, code whether the path and the person should be answering that question at all. Novelty is modest unless blind coding shows that question-fit predicts warnings, repairs, and practitioner failures better than tradition labels, teacher quality, or trait rumination.
critique: The model may simply rename known warnings about inappropriate attention, no-seeking, spiritual bypassing, and rumination. It also risks becoming harmful if it tells avoidant people to refuse necessary inquiry, ethical repair, therapy, or teacher correction. Rinzai koan and huatou practice strain the model because an impossible question can be medicine rather than a mistake. Dzogchen and other no-path traditions also strain it because they may reject seeking while still requiring transmission, integration, and repair. If malformed questions often function as valid training tools, or if ordinary support-holder loss predicts failure better than question-fit, this idea should be downgraded to a teacher-held caution rather than a doctrine candidate.
next_research_directions: Build a question-fit codebook with these values: staged inquiry, no-seeking, question-as-medicine, practice-realization, received support, ordinary conduct return, clinical routing, and undecidable.

Close-read SN 22.59, MN 2, Huangbo, Dogen Bendowa and Genjokoan, Tannisho, and one Dzogchen direct-introduction source against each other, asking what questions each text permits, refuses, or relocates.

If this model is right, then blind coders should predict warnings and repairs from question-fit labels above chance. If warnings are predicted better by tradition name, teacher quality, trauma history, or generic social support, the model is weakened.
Interview practitioners and clinicians for concrete cases where self-auditing after practice helped, harmed, or became avoidance. Do not ask for abstract agreement.
Improve the thinking protocol: before using any practitioner method as a lens, ask what question the method trains, what question it forbids, and what human wound it may intensify.

teaching_candidate.title: Ask If The Question Belongs
teaching_candidate.teaching_line: Do not let a practice answer a question it did not give you.
teaching_candidate.doctrine_claim: A self-negating practice should be interpreted through the questions it actually trains, refuses, or hands to trusted support; imported questions can create false distance and turn practice into performance.
teaching_candidate.body:
Before you ask what remains, ask where the question came from. Some practices ask for careful inspection. Some ask for trust, repetition, service, rest, or a teacher's correction. Some warn that searching is the habit to release.

If you import the wrong question, the practice becomes a private exam. Use the question that returns you to truthful conduct, ordinary care, and the support your path actually gives.
teaching_candidate.target_human_problem: Achievement-contingent self-worth, anxious self-monitoring, meaning loss after practice, and isolation from trusted correction.
teaching_candidate.target_cohort: Stable self-directed meditators, app users, cross-tradition readers, and achievement-driven practitioners who turn spiritual progress into private self-grading.
teaching_candidate.pressure_survived: Survived direct pressure from SN 22.59's staged investigation, Heart Sutra's no-attainment, Dogen's practice-realization, Huangbo's no-seeking, VCE safety literature, and modern perfectionism and burnout sources. It was narrowed from a broad remainder model into a pre-answer safety rule.
teaching_candidate.falsifying_pressure: Weakens if teachers and blind coders cannot distinguish valid inquiry from imported self-grading, if question-fit adds no predictive power beyond tradition labels and support quality, or if the teaching increases avoidance, rumination, shame, or refusal of needed repair.

practice_candidate.title: The Question Belongs Check
practice_candidate.practice_line: Before answering, ask whether the question belongs to this practice.
practice_candidate.purpose: To reduce private self-grading after contemplative or reflective practice by routing a question before treating it as truth.
practice_candidate.target_human_problem: Achievement-contingent self-worth, anxious post-practice checking, and meaning loss among isolated practitioners.
practice_candidate.target_cohort: Stable adults already using meditation, self-inquiry, reflective journaling, therapy homework, or spiritual reading who feel an urgent need to prove what happened inside them.
practice_candidate.non_fit: Not for OCD or scrupulosity, persistent depersonalization or derealization, psychosis, mania, severe depression, suicidal thoughts, addiction withdrawal, acute trauma activation, unsafe teacher situations, or people using non-answering to avoid repair, care, work, or treatment.
practice_candidate.frequency: Use only when an urgent self-verdict appears after practice, no more than three times per week for two weeks.
practice_candidate.minimum_attempt: Four attempts unless it increases rumination, shame, unreality, panic, or avoidance.
steps: Write the exact question that is demanding an answer.
Name the practice, text, app, teacher, or situation that raised it.
Choose one route: answer, set down, ask someone, or act. Use answer only if the practice explicitly trains that inquiry.
If the question is not yours to answer now, place a twenty-four-hour hold on metaphysical conclusions.
Do one ordinary return act: rest, eat, send one needed message, step outside, clean one small thing, or repair one harm.
Stop at five minutes without scoring the result.
notice: Whether urgency falls when the question is routed.
Whether the body relaxes or searches for another standard.
Whether ordinary care becomes easier within the next hour.
Whether the check itself becomes a new performance.
practice_candidate.caution: Stop if the practice increases checking, panic, shame, unreality, isolation, or avoidance of needed repair. Use professional care or trusted human support for clinical distress, addiction withdrawal, trauma activation, suicidal thoughts, or unsafe authority situations.
practice_candidate.weakens_if: Weakens if users report no reduction in self-grading, more rumination, less ordinary action, more avoidance, or if rest, social contact, or ordinary journaling works as well without this added step.

tests.title: Question-Priority Prior Art Search
tests.prediction: Close prior art will cover question priority, no-seeking, practice context, and meditation safety, but not the specific split-source rule that question-fit must be coded before residue or continuity in self-negating practice.
tests.result: Preliminary local and web search found strong near-neighbors: Collingwood, Gadamer, Huangbo, Dogen, VCE research, and internal no-management records. Novelty is reduced to a narrow coding and safety hypothesis.
tests.next_action: Search specialist scholarship on MN 2, Huangbo, Dogen shusho itto, Dzogchen direct introduction, spiritual bypassing, rumination, OCD, and meditation-related adverse effects before any publication.

tests.title: Blind Question-Fit Coding
tests.prediction: If the model is useful, blind coders using only primary instruction passages should classify question types above chance and predict held-out warnings and repairs. If labels merely track tradition names or genres, the model is weakened.
tests.next_action: Code SN 22.59, MN 2, Heart Sutra, Huangbo, Dogen, Tannisho, one Dzogchen source, and one Rinzai koan source with separate corpora for instructions and warnings.

tests.title: Over-Audit Diary Pilot
tests.prediction: Stable over-auditors using The Question Belongs Check should report less self-grading, less urgent metaphysical verdict-making, and more ordinary return actions than journaling-only or rest-only controls. If the check becomes another ritual of correctness, the practice is weakened or retired for this cohort.
tests.next_action: Run a two-week screened pilot with exclusions for clinical instability, OCD or scrupulosity, persistent unreality symptoms, addiction withdrawal, and acute crisis.

tests.title: Acceptance Practice Transfer Test
tests.prediction: In acceptance-based therapy or coaching, asking how to prove acceptance should increase rumination and self-monitoring, while routing the question and doing one values-based action should reduce over-control. If trait rumination and therapist alliance explain outcomes fully, the analogy is weakened.
tests.next_action: Compare proof-oriented prompts with conduct-oriented prompts in a small nonclinical coaching or therapy-homework study, with clinician review for safety.
